BTC-USD.reasoning: BTC is the primary beneficiary of the current crypto sector rotation. BTC dominance is rising to 57.5% as capital flees altcoins into the perceived safe haven — this is the textbook 'rotation into the safe-haven sector' signal. Daily technicals confirm: RSI 73, MACD histogram +634 (strongly positive), all daily EMAs aligned uptrend (10>21>50), ROC_21 +14.2% — the strongest momentum of all five assets. Institutional accumulation is exceptional: Strategy added 13,927 BTC, spot BTC ETFs logged $921M net inflows over 5 days with whale accumulation the largest monthly haul since 2013. Exchange reserves hit 9-year lows (5.88% of supply) — structurally reduced sell-side. The hourly pullback (hourly RSI 31, EMAs downtrend, MACD negative) reflects short-term consolidation from the $77,321 swing high back toward the $73,859-$74,600 support cluster. This is an ideal rotation entry — buy the hourly dip within a daily uptrend with sector tailwinds. Upcoming catalysts: CLARITY Act markup, SEC Regulation Crypto publication, U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve design due July 2026. Key risk: BTC currently extended at 7.84% above 50-SMA and Bollinger position 0.83 — if it fails to hold $73,860 support, a deeper retest of $71,432 (Fib 50%) is possible.

ETH-USD.reasoning: ETH presents a conflicted rotation picture. On the bearish side: BTC dominance rising to 57.5% historically suppresses ETH outperformance; altcoin season index stalled at 34; hourly indicators are deeply oversold and bearish (hourly RSI 25.7, MFI 23.3, EMAs aligned downtrend, volume only 0.59x average) signaling institutional outflows on the short timeframe; the $5.4B Aave rsETH exploit is a structural DeFi risk overhang; ETH is 20% below its 200-day SMA at $2,867 confirming the longer-term rotation away from smart contract platforms. On the bullish side: six consecutive days of ETH ETF inflows, cumulative ETH ETF inflows at record $11.82B, Glamsterdam hard fork (parallel execution, ~78% fee reduction) as a major H1 2026 catalyst, whale accumulation of 7.6M ETH in April, Ethereum Foundation shifted from ETH sales to staking yield (removed sell pressure), and daily MACD/RSI still constructive. The competing forces — ETF rotation in vs. BTC dominance flight-to-safety rotation out — create a neutral cross-signal. ETH needs to reclaim $2,350+ on the hourly to confirm rotation resumption. Holding neutral here until BTC stabilizes and altcoin rotation begins.

SOL-USD.reasoning: SOL is showing the clearest rotation-outflow signal of all assets analyzed. In the sector rotation framework, high-performance L1s like SOL are the highest-beta 'risk-on' sector — they receive capital last in a bull cycle and lose it first when fear rises. All indicators confirm outflows: hourly EMAs all aligned downtrend; hourly RSI 31.2; hourly volume collapsed to just 22.5% of average — the worst volume ratio of any asset in this analysis, indicating near-total institutional disengagement; hourly MACD histogram negative and declining. Daily indicators are mixed (RSI 58, EMAs mixed — NOT aligned uptrend) — no daily bullish confirmation even before the intraday deterioration. ROC_21 +5.1% vs BTC's +14.2% confirms significant relative underperformance. News flow is negative: SOL leading altcoin losses at -3%, $270M Drift exploit security overhang, technical analysts flagging breakdown risk at $78-82 support. Whale accumulation from Coinbase (439K SOL) and a $1B+ spot SOL ETF are positive medium-term catalysts but insufficient to override the current rotation-out signal. The bearish signal is a rotation-based fade, not a fundamental short — the setup targets a retest of $78.95 support with potential for a wider move to $76-77 if BTC dominance continues rising.

BNB-USD.reasoning: BNB sits in an ambiguous rotation zone — underperforming BTC (the dominant sector) but showing marginally better relative strength than SOL or the broader altcoin universe. Exchange tokens like BNB suffer when CEX volumes contract (Q1 2026 CEX volumes down 48% from October peak — a structural headwind), but BNB also has independent utility from BNB Chain DeFi/BSC activity. Technically: daily RSI 63, MACD histogram small positive (+5.37), CCI 165 (elevated); EMAs not aligned up or down — true neutral zone. Hourly is more constructive than ETH/SOL/XRP: hourly RSI 42 (least oversold of the alts), hourly MACD histogram turning positive (+0.38 — recovering from trough), hourly Bollinger position 0.45 (mid-band). Near-term catalysts are real: Osaka/Mendel hard fork April 28, 35th quarterly BNB burn ($1.32B removed), YZi Labs $1B ecosystem fund, Kyrgyzstan CBDC on BNB Chain. However, BNB is trading below its 50-day EMA ($634) and the DOJ probe overhang remains. The rotation signal is ambiguous — BNB is neither a clear recipient of inflows (like BTC) nor a clear outflow target (like SOL). Holding neutral with a slight positive lean if BNB holds above $616 support ahead of the hard fork catalyst.

XRP-USD.reasoning: XRP is the most unusual asset in this basket: it is generating a partial rotation-IN signal driven by regulatory catalysts rather than the typical BTC-dominance cycle. In the crypto rotation framework, XRP occupies the 'payments/regulatory catalyst' sector — and that sector is uniquely hot right now. Evidence for rotation in: XRP reclassified as digital commodity by SEC/CFTC joint framework (March 2026) — removing the primary overhanging legal risk; 7 spot XRP ETF applications in final SEC review (Q2 2026 binary event); CLARITY Act nearing Senate vote with Ripple CEO predicting May passage; XRP ETFs logged $65M+ April inflows with AUM crossing $1B; Japan's Rakuten integrating XRP for 44M users; Ripple/Convera partnership for $190B payments across 200 countries. XRP daily volume is ABOVE average (1.097x) — the only altcoin with above-average volume, confirming active capital inflows. Daily CCI 189 and Bollinger position 0.897 are the most stretched readings in this analysis, indicating momentum. However, the hourly picture shows a healthy pullback after the $1.477 swing high (hourly RSI 29.4, EMAs downtrend) — offering a better entry vs. chasing. Risk: the $119M XRP Coinbase transfer is ambiguous and could represent distribution; CCI/Bollinger overextension on daily suggests momentum is stretched. This is a 'regulatory rotation' trade — the thesis breaks on CLARITY Act delay past May or adverse XRP ETF news.
